name: helpers
description: Settings for helpers community cache participation and DevOps audit configuration.

languageModelTools.name: helpers-inspect-copilot-customization-warnings
languageModelTools.userDescription: Read live VS Code errors, warnings, hover details, and quick fixes for a Copilot customization file's frontmatter tools list.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use this tool when working on Copilot customization files such as .agent.md, .instructions.md, .prompt.md, SKILL.md, or copilot-instructions.md and you need the real VS Code diagnostics for the frontmatter tools list. It reads editor errors, warnings, hover details, and available quick fixes, then returns a readable report directly in chat.

filePath.description: Optional absolute path to a Copilot customization file to inspect. When omitted, the active editor file is used.

languageModelTools.name: helpers-strict-lint
languageModelTools.userDescription: Read live VS Code errors and warnings for any file, folder, or the whole workspace.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use this tool to get real VS Code diagnostics (errors, warnings, info) for any file, folder, or the entire workspace. Pass filePath for a single file, folderPath to scan a directory recursively, or omit both for a full workspace scan. Use severityFilter to limit results to errors-only or warnings-and-above.

filePath.description: Absolute path to a specific file to lint. Omit to scan a folder or the whole workspace.

folderPath.description: Absolute path to a folder. Returns diagnostics for all files within it.

severityFilter.description: Filter by minimum severity. Defaults to 'all'.

gitShellHelpers.checkpoint.autoPush.description: Automatically push to remote after every checkpoint commit.

gitShellHelpers.checkpoint.sign.description: GPG-sign checkpoint commits (requires a GPG key configured in git).

gitShellHelpers.checkpoint.useAI.description: Generate commit messages using AI. When off, -m is required.

gitShellHelpers.checkpoint.model.description: Model to use for AI commit message generation. Leave blank to auto-select the cheapest available model.

gitShellHelpers.customizationInspector.e.description: Enable Strict Linting, a chat tool that reads live VS Code errors, warnings, and quick fixes for Copilot customization files.

gitShellHelpers.sessionMemory.enabled.description: Enable Engram-style session memory. Agents log actions and outcomes to a per-workspace session log with TF-IDF indexing and surprise-weighted retrieval. Helps agents learn from past mistakes and adapt across sessions.

gitShellHelpers.formatControl.bypassOnAg.description: Suppress editor.formatOnSave and editor.codeActionsOnSave while enabled. Prevents Prettier, ESLint auto-fix, and other formatters from running on every agent file save. Use the 'Format All Open Files' command to apply formatting once at the end of a request.

gitShellHelpers.localSubagents.ollama.ho.description: Ollama server URL used by the ollama_subagent and ollama_list_models MCP tools.

gitShellHelpers.localSubagents.ollama.de.description: Default Ollama model tag (e.g. 'llama3.1', 'qwen2.5-coder:14b') used when ollama_subagent is called without an explicit model. Leave blank to require the caller to specify one.

gitShellHelpers.localSubagents.ollama.ma.description: Maximum tool-use rounds the local Ollama sub-agent may run before being forced to summarize and finish.

gitShellHelpers.localSubagents.ollama.ti.description: Total wall-clock timeout for one ollama_subagent invocation.

gitShellHelpers.localSubagents.ollama.al.description: Allow the local Ollama sub-agent to write files inside the workspace. Off by default — enable only when you trust the chosen model.
Allow the local Ollama sub-agent to execute shell commands in the workspace root. Off by default — destructive.

gitShellHelpers.localSubagents.fullSyste.description: Master switch for the system_execute MCP tool. When ON, Copilot can dispatch a local Ollama agent that has FULL system access: unrestricted shell, full filesystem, and a persistent Playwright browser. Use this to let a free local model autonomously perform concrete tasks (log into a site to fetch creds, drive a UI, run a long pipeline). Requires Playwright (`npm install -g playwright && npx playwright install chromium`).

gitShellHelpers.localSubagents.systemExe.description: Default Ollama model for system_execute. Use a vision-capable model (e.g. qwen2.5vl:7b, llava:13b, llama3.2-vision:11b) so the agent can see browser screenshots.
Hard cap on agent loop iterations for one system_execute call.
Wall-clock timeout for one system_execute call.
Run the system_execute Playwright browser headless. Turn off if you want to watch the agent work.
Playwright channel for system_execute (chrome, msedge, or chromium for the bundled browser).

Persistent profile directory for the system_execute browser. Empty = use ~/.cache/helpers/playwright-profile (recommended). Point at your real Chrome profile (~/Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome/Default on macOS) ONLY when Chrome is closed.
